description_long: This is a vintage-style brass and wood telescope mounted on a classic tripod stand. The tripod features three slender, adjustable wooden legs with brass fittings, providing stability and an elegant appearance. The telescope tube is cylindrical, predominantly brass with a reddish-brown finish, and includes a small finder scope attached near the eyepiece. The head of the tripod has a central mounting mechanism that allows the telescope to pivot and rotate for aiming. The overall design is reminiscent of 19th-century scientific instruments, with visible screws and joints for adjustment. The object exudes an antique aesthetic, suitable for display in urban parks, observatories, or as a decorative piece in offices or libraries.
description: A vintage brass and wood telescope mounted on a classic three-legged tripod, featuring adjustable legs and an antique finish.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the brass telescope and the three wooden tripod legs, with the finder scope and adjustment joints visible.
description_view_1: Front view looking directly into the telescope's main lens, with the tripod legs symmetrically spread and the mounting head centered.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the telescope tube, tripod structure, and the brass fittings on the legs.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the eyepiece of the telescope and the converging tripod legs, with the mounting mechanism visible at the top.
